text: Last class you disagreed with a stakeholder and reached a compromise.
Today you present that decision to the board in clear, formal English.
Overview
Highlight
Outcome
Bottleneck
Takeaway
Roadmap
Benchmark
Insight
The login feature is ready. However, the testing phase was slow.
The costs stayed in budget. As a result, the board was happy.
We found a bottleneck in testing. Therefore, the next sprint starts late.
The numbers are strong. In addition, the client is satisfied.
Thank you, Rafael. Your overview was clear. What is the main outcome of the project?
The main outcome is positive. We delivered the feature on time. However, the testing phase was slow.
I see. And what is your main takeaway for the board?
My main takeaway is simple. The project is healthy, and the roadmap is realistic. Therefore, the risk is small.
Good. Can you compare the delay to a benchmark from a past project?
Yes. Compared to our benchmark, this delay is normal. In addition, my insight from the data is that quality improved.
Let me give you a brief overview of the project.
The main highlights are strong, and the numbers support them.
However, we found a bottleneck in the testing phase.
As a result, the roadmap now shows new dates and a clear outcome.
To summarize, my main takeaway is that the project is healthy.

Hi, this is Rafael. I am recording a quick rehearsal of my board presentation for tomorrow. Let me walk you through the plan. First, I will give a short overview of the project in one minute. Then I will share two highlights. The team delivered the login feature on time, and the costs stayed inside the budget. However, I also want to be honest about one bottleneck. The testing phase was slower than expected. As a result, the next sprint will start two days late. Therefore, I will show a clear roadmap with the new dates and the expected outcome. To summarize, my main takeaway for the board is simple. The project is healthy, the risk is small, and the plan is realistic. I will keep my tone calm and formal, and I will use data, not opinions. If they ask hard questions, I will answer with one insight from the numbers and stay professional.

Hi Rafael, this is Patricia from the board. Thank you for your presentation this morning. Overall, the outcome was very positive, and the directors were impressed. I want to share three points of feedback. First, your overview was clear and short, which is exactly what an executive audience needs. In addition, your highlights were strong, because you supported them with real numbers, not opinions. Your main takeaway was easy to remember. However, I have one suggestion. When you talked about the bottleneck in testing, you could compare it to a benchmark from a past project, so the board can see if the delay is normal. As a result, your insight will sound even more convincing. To summarize, the roadmap is solid and the message is professional. Please send me the final slides today, and I will share them with the rest of the board before our next meeting. Well done.

First, open with a short overview of your main points. Next, present two or three highlights with clear numbers. Then show the roadmap and the expected outcome of the project. After that, name any bottleneck and share your main insight from the data. Finally, close with one clear takeaway and confirm the next step. Use formal connectors like however, therefore, and to summarize to link your ideas.

Let me give you a brief overview of the project.
The feature is ready. However, testing was slow.
We found a bottleneck. Therefore, the dates change.
My main takeaway is that the project is healthy.
Compared to our benchmark, this delay is normal.
My insight from the data is that quality improved.
The feature is ready. However, the testing phase was slow.
We found a bottleneck. Therefore, the next sprint starts late.
To summarize, my main takeaway is that the project is healthy.
The main highlights are strong, and the numbers support them.
However, we found a bottleneck in the testing phase.
As a result, the roadmap now shows new dates and a clear outcome.
